title: Plug Power Stock Pops on Earnings. The Hydrogen Company Improved Its Margins 71%.
text: Plug Power (PLUG) delivered impressive Q1 FY2026 results on May 11, driving the stock up 12.8% on the announcement day. The hydrogen fuel cell company demonstrated significant operational progress, particularly in profitability metrics. The company achieved a dramatic turnaround in gross margins, with GAAP gross margin improving to -13% from -55% year-over-year—a 71% overall margin improvement. This 42-percentage-point year-over-year expansion was driven by three main factors: operating leverage from sales growth, improved service business unit economics with quarterly per unit costs falling 30% year-over-year, and a 54-percentage-point improvement in fuel margin rates. Revenue grew 22.3% year-over-year to $163.5 million, exceeding analyst expectations of $142.5 million. The electrolyzer segment showed particularly strong momentum, posting a 343% revenue increase to $40.8 million. Rather than vague projections, management established a concrete target: positive EBITDA by Q4 2026. CEO Jose Luis Crespo attributed sustainable margin expansion to reduced liquefied hydrogen costs and improved electrolyzer pricing. Analyst projections suggest continued improvement, with FY2026 loss per share expected to narrow 51.6% year-over-year to $0.30, and FY2027 estimates showing another 40% contraction to $0.18. While the broader analyst community maintains an overall Hold rating, the stock has gained 293.98% over the past 52 weeks and currently trades at a 6.13x forward sales multiple.
